etymology.description: 禾(禾) (hé, 'grain crop') with an extra stroke/something drooping corrupting into 木 (mù, 'tree') + 乃(乃) (nǎi, 'to be') -> bottom part of 禾(禾) becomes 木(木) -> final form 朵
etymology.interpretation: The character started as an ideogram depicting a drooping plant on a stalk, symbolizing the way something hangs, which relates to the shape of an earlobe. Thus, 朵 is used for 'earlobe' and metaphorically for 'measure word for flowers/clouds' because they also hang or droop in form.

examples.chinese: 天空中飘着几朵白云(天空中飄着幾朵白雲)。
examples.pinyin: Tiānkōng zhōng piāo zhe jǐ duǒ báiyún.
examples.english: A few white clouds are floating in the sky.

examples.chinese: 她头上戴着一朵红玫瑰(她頭上戴着一朵紅玫瑰)。
examples.pinyin: Tā tóushàng dài zhe yī duǒ hóng méigui.
examples.english: She is wearing a red rose on her head.

examples.chinese: 海边有朵朵浪花(海邊有朵朵浪花)，非常美丽(非常美麗)。
examples.pinyin: Hǎibiān yǒu duǒ duǒ lànghuā, fēicháng měilì.
examples.english: There are waves of flowers by the seaside, which are very beautiful.
